2026-04-22T06:43:07.9534066Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T06:43:07.9534643Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T06:43:07.9535108Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T06:43:07.9535502Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m

2026-04-22T06:54:27.5596286Z

2026-04-22T06:54:27.5597292Z   
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5598348Z   Now I'm comparing the old beta's API version against what's in this PR. The old `_meta.json` was generated with autorest but didn't explicitly specify an API version, while the new metadata shows `2024-11-01`. Looking at the diff to understand what version was actually in use before and how the TypeSpec generation differs.
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5599582Z 
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5600914Z ✓ $ cat /tmp/gh-aw/mcp-payloads/***/1073cd95daa31e6bf5ccf64d0dea9e8e/payload.json | python3 -c " import json, sys data = json.load(sys.stdin) text = data[0]['content'][0]['text'] # Look for the old API file (removed lines) idx1 = text.find('diff --g...
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5601759Z 
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5601950Z ◆ Looking at the old API, the constructor signature was:
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5602455Z   - `constructor(credentials: coreAuth.TokenCredential, subscriptionId: string, options?: ...)`
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5603013Z   - `constructor(credentials: coreAuth.TokenCredential, options?: ...)`
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5603359Z   
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5603524Z   And the new:
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5603788Z   - `constructor(credential: TokenCredential, options?: ...)`
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5604259Z   - `constructor(credential: TokenCredential, subscriptionId: string, options?: ...)`
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5604646Z   
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5604927Z   Note that in the new version, the parameter order for the two overloads is:
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5605321Z   1. `credential, options` (no subscriptionId)
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5605624Z   2. `credential, subscriptionId, options`
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5606316Z   
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5606506Z   In the old version, the order was:
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5606753Z   
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5606904Z   
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5607100Z   1. `credentials, subscriptionId, options`
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5607406Z   2. `credentials, options` (no subscriptionId)
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5607671Z   
2026-04-22T06:54:27.5608666Z   Both versions support both signatures, so this is a valid API change. The old package had `apiVersion: string` as a field within `CertificateRegistrationManagementClientOptionalParams`, without an explicit `KnownVersions` enum, so there's no version comparison issue to worry about.
